$description: iAsBuilt Semantic Color Tokens — Light Mode

default.$description: Top-level app chrome background; the main canvas behind shell UI.
Slightly tinted variant of the app background for subtle zoning.
Primary content canvas where documents and panels are rendered.
Recessed area within a canvas, e.g. inset toolbars or wells.
Page-level background framing the canvas; the lowest layer behind app content.
Dimming overlay placed behind modals and sheets to focus attention.
Tinted backdrop layer applied beneath floating surfaces.
Inverted background used for high-contrast surfaces like tooltips.
Default surface tier for cards, panels and content blocks.
Lightly tinted surface used to differentiate adjacent regions.
Recessed surface tier suggesting depth below the base layer.
Elevated surface tier for cards lifted above the base layer.
Surface tier for floating UI such as menus and dropdowns.
Surface tier for modal dialogs and full overlays.
Surface tier for popovers and richer detached panels.
Inverted surface tier for tooltips to ensure strong contrast.
High-contrast inverted surface for emphasis blocks.
Tinted surface in the brand hue for soft brand context.
Saturated surface in the brand hue for prominent brand context.
Surface tier indicating a disabled or inactive region.
Surface tier indicating a selected or active region.
Primary body text; the highest-contrast text role.
Secondary text for supporting copy and labels.
Tertiary text for de-emphasized helper content.
Muted text for the lowest-priority readable text.
Text color used on disabled controls and content.
Inverted text used on dark or saturated surfaces.
Text used directly on top of brand-strong surfaces.
Default link color in body text.

hover.$description: Link color on hover; one tonal step deeper than default.

visited.$description: Visited link color in a complementary purple-blue.

default.$description: Text color conveying a successful or positive state.
Text color conveying caution or pending attention.
Text color conveying an error or destructive state.
Text color conveying informational context.
Primary icon color matching primary text emphasis.
Secondary icon color for supporting glyphs.
Tertiary icon color for low-emphasis glyphs.
Icon color for disabled or inactive controls.
Inverted icon color for dark or brand-strong surfaces.
Icon color used directly on brand-strong surfaces.
Icon color conveying success or completion.
Icon color conveying caution or pending state.
Icon color conveying error or destructive intent.
Icon color conveying informational context.
Subtle border for low-emphasis separation.
Default border for inputs, cards and containers.
Strong border for emphasis or active outlines.
Inverted border for use on dark surfaces.
Border indicating keyboard focus on a control.
Border in the brand hue used for primary emphasis.
Border conveying a successful state.
Border conveying a warning state.
Border conveying an error or destructive state.
Border conveying an informational state.
Border color for disabled or inactive controls.
Subtle horizontal or vertical divider line.
Default divider line for sectioning content.
Strong divider line for high-emphasis separation.
Focus ring color used around focused controls.
Inset focus ring applied inside the control bounds.
Focus outline drawn outside the control bounds.
Background color for selected text or items.
Border color framing a selected item.
Text color used inside selected regions.
Drag handle color shown on selected ranges.
Ambient shadow color used for soft surrounding lift.
Key directional shadow giving primary depth cue.
Stronger shadow used beneath floating overlays.
Primary iAsBuilt brand blue used for prominent actions.
